_meta.principle: Producers who shape DHH equally with the rappers. Verified credits via Wikipedia, label sites, Genius credits.

producers.name: Sez on the Beat
producers.blurb: The Delhi producer who scored the conscious wave. Class-Sikh (Prabh Deep), Little Kid Big Dreams (Ahmer), early Divine and Naezy tracks. Sample-based, jazz-influenced, the most patient ear in DHH.

producers.name: Karan Kanchan
producers.blurb: Trap with Indian textures. Produced for Divine, Raja Kumari, Naezy, and his own catalogue. The Mumbai bridge between gully and global trap.

producers.name: Stunnah Beatz
producers.blurb: KR$NA's primary collaborator. Produced No Cap, Untitled, Joota Japani. The pen-game wave needed a producer who could match the lyric density.

producers.name: Kalmi
producers.blurb: Seedhe Maut's primary in-house producer. The texture under Lunch Break, NAYAAB, Shaktimaan. Atmospheric boom-bap with melodic threads.

producers.name: Kushagra
producers.blurb: Yashraj and the new wave's preferred producer. Soft on the ear, hard on the construction. Bombay Coast, Ishq Nachaawe all carry his fingerprint.

producers.name: KRAZY
producers.blurb: The Punjabi wave's quiet engine. Beats for Karan Aujla and rotating Punjabi A-listers. Drill plus traditional dhol — the modern Punjabi sound.
